skill.name: vector-index-tuning
skill.description: Optimize vector index performance for latency, recall, and memory by tuning HNSW settings, quantization, and search benchmarks.
skill.summary: Tune vector indexes with practical benchmarks, parameter guidance, and production safety checks.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, filesystem, and weak-cryptography patterns, but review found these are benign documentation artifacts. The skill contains Markdown guidance and Python examples for vector index tuning; no malicious intent, prompt injection, credential access, or data exfiltration was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Formatting Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections occur on Markdown backticks, code fence boundaries, and inline resource references. The reviewed content shows Python examples and documentation links, not shell execution or command injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown fences, inline backticks, or boundaries around Python examples. No shell command invocation or user-controlled command construction was found at these locations.

low_findings.title: Reference Links Misidentified as Runtime Network Access
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are resource links to the HNSW paper, Faiss wiki, and ANN Benchmarks. They are not fetched by code and do not transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are plain Markdown links in a resources section. There is no code path that performs HTTP requests or sends local data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Vector Database Disk Thresholds Misidentified as File Access
low_findings.description: The memory-mapped file detections are Qdrant optimizer configuration examples using memmap_threshold values. They describe vector storage tuning and do not read arbitrary files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are configuration parameters inside a Qdrant collection example. They can affect storage behavior if a user applies the example, but they are not standalone filesystem access routines.

low_findings.title: Quantization Code Misidentified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections correspond to vector quantization arrays, KMeans codes, and skill description text. No cryptographic hash, encryption, or authentication logic is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The implementation lines use numeric codes for product quantization, not cryptographic primitives. The SKILL.md lines describe vector search scope and contain no crypto operations.

content.value_statement: Vector search systems often miss latency, recall, or memory targets after launch. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan benchmarks, tune HNSW settings, and choose quantization strategies.
actual_capabilities: Guides HNSW parameter selection for recall, latency, and build time tradeoffs.
Provides benchmark templates for measuring recall, latency percentiles, QPS, and memory use.
Explains scalar, product, binary, FP16, and FP32 quantization tradeoffs.
Shows Qdrant collection configuration patterns for recall, speed, balanced, and memory goals.
Supports production rollout planning with staging validation and rollback checks.
limitations: Does not design a full retrieval application or ranking pipeline.
Requires representative queries and ground truth data for reliable recall analysis.
Example code must be adapted and tested against the user database version.
Does not replace load testing on production-like infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Reduce Search Latency
use_cases.description: Tune efSearch, M, and quantization settings to lower p95 latency while preserving target recall.

use_cases.title: Plan Memory Reduction
use_cases.description: Compare FP32, FP16, INT8, product quantization, and binary storage before resizing vector infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Validate Index Changes
use_cases.description: Build a repeatable benchmark that tracks recall, latency, QPS, and memory before deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Starting Index
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a first vector index configuration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to recommend a starting vector index type. My dataset has [number] vectors, [dimensions] dimensions, and [latency target]. Explain the tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Tune HNSW Parameters
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate tuning for recall and latency.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to tune HNSW parameters for [database or library]. Target recall is [value], p95 latency is [value], and memory budget is [value].

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Quantization
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to compare quantization options for [embedding model] vectors. Include expected memory impact, recall risk, and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Benchmark Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a benchmark and rollout plan for changing vector index settings in production. Include metrics, parameter sweeps, staging checks, and rollback criteria.

output_examples.input: Tune a one million vector HNSW index for 95 percent recall and low latency.
output_examples.output: A parameter sweep plan covering M, efConstruction, and efSearch, with recall and latency metrics to compare each run.

output_examples.input: Reduce memory use for a large semantic search index.
output_examples.output: A quantization comparison that explains FP16, INT8, product quantization, and binary options with validation risks.

output_examples.input: Prepare a production rollout for new Qdrant index settings.
output_examples.output: A staged checklist with baseline metrics, load testing, recall checks, monitoring, and rollback thresholds.

best_practices: Benchmark with real queries and ground truth labels before choosing final settings.
Tune one major dimension at a time so recall, latency, and memory changes are attributable.
Validate index changes in staging under realistic load before production rollout.
anti_patterns: Do not optimize only for latency without measuring recall loss.
Do not apply quantization globally before testing representative query classes.
Do not reindex production data without capacity planning and rollback criteria.

faq.question: What vector indexes does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It focuses on HNSW, flat indexes, IVF, product quantization, and DiskANN-style scaling decisions.

faq.question: Can it tune Qdrant configurations?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Qdrant-oriented examples for HNSW, quantization, optimizer thresholds, and search parameters.

faq.question: Does it need production data?
faq.answer: It needs representative vectors, queries, and ground truth labels. Staging data is preferred for safe testing.

faq.question: Will it guarantee a specific recall level?
faq.answer: No. It provides a benchmark process so recall can be measured and tuned for the workload.

faq.question: Is this useful for small datasets?
faq.answer: Only sometimes. Small datasets often work best with flat exact search instead of approximate indexes.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex implement the examples?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples can guide implementation, but database versions and local test results must be verified.
